Augusta, WI 54722

citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
A male poodle aged 3-4 years old (#16) had hair loss on its lower back. The hair loss has been there for a few months
and according to the licensee, the hair loss has increased over time. The licensee has not contacted the attending
veterinarian. Hair loss can indicate underlying health issues. It can be irritating and can potentially even be caused by
something that is contagious to other dogs or even people. The licensee needs to contact the attending veterinarian and
initiate treatment per his/her instructions. Correct by 2/28/26.

Sixteen puppies in the kennel had no identification. Not having proper identification on all puppies doesn't allow for APHIS
officials to properly track the movement of the dogs through the kennel. The licensee must identify all puppies in the
kennel in accordance with the Animal Welfare Act. Correct starting on 3/4/26.

An enclosure containing an adult female and three puppies had carpeting in it that was frayed and had not been changed
out in five weeks. The carpeting was filthy and discolored from the fecal material on it. Not replacing the carpeting when it
can't be sufficiently cleaned can contribute to the spread of disease. The carpeting must be replaced or removed. Correct
starting on 2/25/26.

The container inside the kennel that stores the bedding did not have a lid on it and a container located outdoors that
contains bedding did not have a lid that covered the entire opening and snow had accumulated on the bedding. Not
storing the bedding properly can result in the bedding becoming contaminated which can result in the spread of disease.
Storage containers for bedding must have a properly fitting lid to prevent contamination. Correct starting on 2/28/26.

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
When entering the kennel, there is a strong smell of ammonia. The puppy cages were dirty and had a good amount of
fecal material caking the grates and surfaces that the dogs were laying on. Not sanitizing all enclosures every two weeks
or more often as necessary can contribute to the spread of disease. All enclosures must be sanitized every two weeks or
more often as necessary. Correct by 3/4/26.

One adult dog has not been vaccinated against distemper or parvo in the past year and a half. Two cages of puppies (6
total puppies) didn't get their parvo vaccines and dewormer in accordance with the schedule from the attending
veterinarian. In addition, no dogs have been sampled for fecal parasites in the past year. Not vaccinating all adults
dogs/puppies, not deworming puppies, and not sampling the adult dogs for fecal parasites doesn't ensure the dogs are
adequately protected from various diseases and parasites. All adult dogs must be vaccinated for distemper and parvo, all
puppies must be dewormed and vaccinated in accordance with the schedule from the attending veterinarian, and the
population must be sampled for parasites in accordance with the plan from the attending veterinarian. Correct by 3/25/26.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
End of report.
Additional Inspectors:
KELLY MAXWELL n

narrative:
2.40(b)(2) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
A male poodle aged 3-4 years old (#16) had hair loss on its lower back. The hair loss has been there for a few months

something that is contagious to other dogs or even people. The licensee needs to contact the attending veterinarian and

2.50(a)(2) 
   
Time and method of identification. 
Sixteen puppies in the kennel had no identification. Not having proper identification on all puppies doesn't allow for APHIS 
officials to properly track the movement of the dogs through the kennel. The licensee must identify all puppies in the 
kennel in accordance with the Animal Welfare Act. Correct starting on 3/4/26. 
3.1(c)(3) 
   
Housing facilities, general. 
An enclosure containing an adult female and three puppies had carpeting in it that was frayed and had not been changed

can't be sufficiently cleaned can contribute to the spread of disease. The carpeting must be replaced or removed. Correct

Housing facilities, general. 
The container inside the kennel that stores the bedding did not have a lid on it and a container located outdoors that 
contains bedding did not have a lid that covered the entire opening and snow had accumulated on the bedding. Not 
storing the bedding properly can result in the bedding becoming contaminated which can result in the spread of disease. 
Storage containers for bedding must have a properly fitting lid to prevent contamination. Correct starting on 2/28/26. 
3.11(b)(2) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.

When entering the kennel, there is a strong smell of ammonia. The puppy cages were dirty and had a good amount of 
fecal material caking the grates and surfaces that the dogs were laying on. Not sanitizing all enclosures every two weeks 
or more often as necessary can contribute to the spread of disease. All enclosures must be sanitized every two weeks or

3.13(a)(3) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
One adult dog has not been vaccinated against distemper or parvo in the past year and a half. Two cages of puppies (6 
total puppies) didn't get their parvo vaccines and dewormer in accordance with the schedule from the attending 
veterinarian. In addition, no dogs have been sampled for fecal parasites in the past year. Not vaccinating all adults

population must be sampled for parasites in accordance with the plan from the attending veterinarian. Correct by 3/25/26. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee. 
 
End of report.
